metadata.title: States PCS: Nagaland GK Questions for Nagaland PSC Examinations

questions.question: Which tribe is historically credited with founding the traditional village of Khonoma in Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Khonoma is an Angami Naga village in Nagaland, famous for its resistance to British rule and as India’s first green village.

questions.question: Which British political officer was killed during the 1879 Naga uprising at Khonoma in present-day Nagaland?
questions.explanation: G.H. Damant, a British political officer, was killed at Khonoma by Nagas in 1879, prompting a major British military response.

questions.question: Who is credited with introducing Christianity to the Naga Hills in the late 19th century?
questions.explanation: Rev. Edward Winter Clark, an American Baptist missionary, introduced Christianity to the Naga Hills in 1872 and promoted modern education among the Nagas.

questions.question: Which organization was established in 1918 to represent the interests of the Nagas to the British government?
questions.explanation: The Naga Club was formed in 1918 by educated Nagas and later submitted a memorandum to the Simon Commission in 1929, seeking to remain independent after British rule.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Naga National Council (NNC)?
questions.explanation: Angami Zapu Phizo was the main founder and leader of NNC, instrumental in the Naga independence movement.

questions.question: In which year did the Naga National Council (NNC) proclaim independence from British India?
questions.explanation: The NNC declared Naga independence on 14 August 1947, a day before India’s independence, marking the start of the Naga political movement.

questions.question: Which agreement was signed in 1975 between the Government of India and a faction of the Naga National Council (NNC)?
questions.explanation: The Shillong Accord was signed in 1975 between the Indian government and a section of the NNC, leading to disarmament of some Naga insurgents and new insurgent groups.

questions.question: Which militant group was formed in 1980 by leaders opposing the Shillong Accord in Nagaland?
questions.explanation: The NSCN was established in 1980 by Isak Chishi Swu, Thuingaleng Muivah, and others who opposed the Shillong Accord; it later split into the NSCN-IM and NSCN-K factions.

questions.question: In which year did Nagaland officially become a state of India?
questions.explanation: Nagaland attained statehood on 1 December 1963 as the 16th state of India after negotiations and enactment of Article 371(A).

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution grants special autonomy and safeguards to the state of Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Article 371A provides Nagaland special autonomy regarding religious and social practices, customary law, and land ownership.
